hints: P(M_n ≤ ln n)=P(all X_i ≤ ln n)=(1−e^{−ln n})ⁿ.
solution: $P(M_n\le\ln n)=\prod P(X_i\le\ln n)=(1-e^{-\ln n})^n=(1-\tfrac1n)^n\to e^{-1}=\tfrac1e$. So $b=1.$

hints: A triangle fails iff one side exceeds the sum of the other two; the three failure events are disjoint.
Each has probability 1/6 by symmetry (e.g. P(X>Y+Z)=1/6).
solution: No triangle forms iff one length exceeds the other two combined; these three events are disjoint and each has probability $\tfrac16$ (e.g. $P(X>Y+Z)=\tfrac16$). So $P(\text{triangle})=1-3\cdot\tfrac16=\tfrac12.$

hints: Write X=Z₁, Y=ρZ₁+√(1−ρ²)Z₂ with Z₁,Z₂ IID N(0,1).
Cov(X,Y²)=E[Z₁Y²]; expand Y² — all terms involve odd moments of Z₁ (or E[Z₂])=0.
Cov(X,Y²)=0.
solution: With $X=Z_1$, $Y=\rho Z_1+\sqrt{1-\rho^2}Z_2$: $\mathbb{E}[XY^2]=\rho^2\mathbb{E}[Z_1^3]+2\rho\sqrt{1-\rho^2}\mathbb{E}[Z_1^2 Z_2]+(1-\rho^2)\mathbb{E}[Z_1 Z_2^2]=0$ (each term has $\mathbb{E}[Z_1^3]=0$ or $\mathbb{E}[Z_2]=0$). Since $\mathbb{E}[X]=0$, $\mathrm{Cov}(X,Y^2)=0.$

hints: By symmetry the answer is twice the expected tosses for Anna (3 heads) to win.
Anna wins in 3,4,5 tosses with probabilities 1/8, 3/16, 6/32.
For two IID uniforms on 1..n, E[|X−Y|]=(n²−1)/(3n).
Plug n=30.
solution: For two independent uniform rolls on $1,\dots,n$, $\mathbb{E}[|X-Y|]=\dfrac{n^2-1}{3n}$. With $n=30$ this is $\dfrac{899}{90}.$

hints: Fix the red value; each blue matches it independently with probability 1/6.
Exactly one of two blues matches: C(2,1)(1/6)(5/6).
solution: Given the red value, each blue die matches it with probability $\tfrac16$. Exactly one of the two blues matching is $\binom21\tfrac16\cdot\tfrac56=\tfrac{10}{36}=\tfrac{5}{18}.$

hints: With n noodles (2n ends), each join closes a loop with probability 1/(2k−1) when 2k ends remain.
solution: When $2k$ ends remain, picking a first end, the chance its partner is its own other end (closing a loop) is $\tfrac1{2k-1}$. By linearity $\mathbb{E}[\text{circles}]=\sum_{k=1}^{100}\tfrac1{2k-1}\approx3.28$, which rounds to $3.$

hints: Underlying = product of two dice; call pays max(X−30,0).
Only X=36 (6×6) exceeds 30, paying 6.
solution: The call on the product pays $\max(X-30,0)$, nonzero only when $X=36$ (the roll $6,6$), paying $6$. So the price is $6\cdot\tfrac1{36}=\tfrac16.$

hints: Count non-crossing perfect matchings of 6 points vs all matchings.
Non-crossing matchings = Catalan C₃ = 5; total matchings = 5!! = 15.
solution: The number of non-crossing perfect matchings of $6$ points on a circle is the Catalan number $C_3=5$, out of $5!!=15$ total matchings. So $P=\tfrac{5}{15}=\tfrac13.$

hints: After the first pass, the survivors are the even numbers; the pattern repeats on a halved set.
The last number deleted is the highest power of 2 within the range.
Largest power of 2 ≤ 2000 is 1024.
solution: Each pass deletes every other number, so after the first pass only multiples of $2$ remain, then multiples of $4$, and so on. The number surviving longest (last deleted) is the largest power of $2$ in range, $2^{10}=1024.$

hints: Use the hedge weights from ρ=−1: w₁=σ₂/(σ₁+σ₂)≈0.341, w₂≈0.659.
Portfolio return = 0.04w₁ + 0.02w₂.
solution: The risk-free weights are $w_1=\dfrac{\sigma_2}{\sigma_1+\sigma_2}\approx0.341$, $w_2\approx0.659$. The portfolio's expected return is $0.04(0.341)+0.02(0.659)\approx0.0268$, i.e. $2.68\%.$

hints: E[X]=1/2; compute E[X²]=∫₀¹x²dx=1/3.
